2024-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: Announced Perfect Day Mexico for 2027 opening, positioned to serve both Eastern and Western Caribbean itineraries. Still in design and planning phase with CocoCay-style premium experiences (Hideaway, Beach Club, slides) expected.

status_delta.detail: first quarter this asset appears in the lifecycle record

key_uncertainties: incremental cost cadence through build period
2024-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Reaffirmed 2027 launch with private-destination penetration rising to 90% of Royal Caribbean Caribbean guests by 2027. Costa Maya port acquisition driving near-term cost ramp and significant share of 2025 non-ship capex.

key_uncertainties: timing and magnitude of Costa Maya cost ramp within the year
2025-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Limited update; Costa Maya port acquisition and other destination ramp-up cited as a driver of cost timing across dry docks and ship deliveries.

2025-Q2.mgmt_statement_summary: Closed on Costa Maya port acquisition and began build-out; late-2027 debut reaffirmed with footprint described as roughly the size of Magic Kingdom. Positioned as a 2028 full-year operational benefit within Perfecta.

key_uncertainties: yield vs onboard-spend split as the asset opens
2025-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: Build-out described as a transformational development supporting expansion of exclusive land-based destinations from two to eight by 2028. Expected to shift Royal Caribbean's revenue mix more toward a balance of ticket and onboard versus CocoCay.

key_uncertainties: 2026 non-ship capex allocation across Santorini, Cozumel, and Perfect Day Mexico
2025-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Perfect Day Mexico flagged as a significant share of $1.8B non-ship capex and a key driver of lifting private-destination guest penetration to 90% by 2028.

key_uncertainties: structural Western Caribbean yield uplift sizing
2026-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Late-2027 opening reaffirmed with ramp into early 2028, alongside Royal Beach Club Cozumel now pushed to early 2028. Positioned with Cozumel and Icon Class ships as the centerpiece of the Western Caribbean strategy.

asked_by_history.framing: Gross capital investment level and return expectations for the newly announced project
Potential yield uplift for Western Caribbean itineraries by analogy to CocoCay's Eastern Caribbean impact
Western Caribbean mix expansion and eventual passenger throughput at Perfect Day Mexico
Whether 2028 earnings growth can at least match Perfecta targets given Perfect Day Mexico and other 2028 launches
Clarification on Costa Maya being Magic Kingdom-sized and what amenities that footprint implies
Footprint scale of Perfect Day Mexico and guest density relative to the Magic Kingdom comparison
Whether Costa Maya costs are already embedded in 3Q NCC guidance
Whether $5B annual CapEx and the non-new-build mix is a fair run-rate through 2026-2027 given Costa Maya
Cadence of the 2028 ramp and structural Western Caribbean yield opportunity including Galveston penetration

primary_framings_observed: CapEx sizing and CapEx cadence into 2026-2027
Yield uplift sizing by analogy to CocoCay
Western Caribbean mix shift and drive-market (Galveston) penetration
Scale, capacity, and amenity mix of the destination

currently_open_questions_as_of_2026Q1: Quantitative ramp cadence in 2028 (utilization, calls per week, guest throughput)
Sizing of the structural Western Caribbean yield uplift versus CocoCay's Eastern Caribbean precedent
Galveston and broader Texas drive-market penetration opportunity attributable to the asset
Total project capex disclosure and per-guest unit economics at maturity
Ticket-versus-onboard revenue mix expected at Perfect Day Mexico relative to CocoCay
Margin trajectory through the remaining 2026-2027 build-out before revenue contribution begins
